title: Detect beads (br) and agent-mail availability at orchestrator startup
description: In orch_profile (or a new init phase in /orchestrate command), check if 'br' CLI is installed and .beads/ exists, and if agent-mail MCP server is reachable (curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/). Store availability flags in OrchestratorState (hasBeads, hasAgentMail) alongside existing hasSophia. When both are available, prefer beads+agent-mail over sophia for coordination. Add types to types.ts and detection logic.

title: Extract commands into src/commands.ts
description:
Extract the 4 command handlers from index.ts into src/commands.ts.

## Commands to extract (index.ts lines ~311-496):
- /orchestrate (line ~311)
- /orchestrate-stop (line ~344) 
- /orchestrate-status (line ~364)
- /memory (line ~376)

## Pattern
Each command becomes an exported function:
```typescript
import type { OrchestratorContext } from './types.js';
export function registerCommands(oc: OrchestratorContext) {
  oc.pi.registerCommand('orchestrate', { ... });
  // etc
}
```

The function takes OrchestratorContext and registers all commands.
index.ts calls registerCommands(oc) after creating the context.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] src/commands.ts exists with all 4 command handlers
- [ ] index.ts imports and calls registerCommands()
- [ ] Command handlers access state/pi/etc via OrchestratorContext
- [ ] npm run build passes
- [ ] npm test passes

### Files: src/commands.ts, src/index.ts

title: Step 5: Final cleanup: slim index.ts to registration-only wiring (imports, state init, pi.on() hooks, registerTool/registerCommand delegations). Remove dead code, unused imports. Verify index.ts is under 300 lines. Update docs/architecture.md to document the new module structure.
description:
Acceptance criteria:
- src/index.ts is under 300 lines
- npm run build passes
- npm test passes
- docs/architecture.md updated with new module structure
- No behavior changes — all tools, commands, hooks work identically

title: Epic: Replace PlanStep with beads+agent-mail as core execution substrate
description:
Fundamental architectural shift: beads (br CLI) become THE planning and execution primitive. Agent-mail handles all coordination. PlanStep type and orch_plan tool are removed entirely.

## Why
- The orchestrator currently has its own PlanStep abstraction that duplicates what beads already do (tasks, deps, status lifecycle)
- Agent-mail provides file reservations and messaging that the orchestrator reimplements with worktree hacks
- The Idea Wizard skill creates beads natively — forcing ideas through PlanStep is a lossy translation
- Beads are git-visible, crash-recoverable, and queryable via br CLI

## Architecture after migration
- Discovery (all modes) → idea generation → br create beads with deps
- Planning IS bead creation — no separate orch_plan tool
- Execution reads br ready --json for actionable beads
- Parallel groups derived from bead dep graph
- orch_review works with bead IDs, br close on pass
- Agent-mail threads scoped to bead IDs for review trails
- br sync --flush-only at every state transition

## What gets removed
- PlanStep type, Plan type
- orch_plan tool entirely  
- analyzeParallelGroups() (replaced by br dep graph)
- createBeadsFromPlan() (beads are created directly, not derived from PlanSteps)

title: Add Bead types and new state fields to types.ts (additive, no removals)
description:
Phase 1: ADD new bead-centric types alongside existing ones (don't remove PlanStep yet — that breaks everything).

Add to types.ts:
- Bead interface matching br list --json output: { id, title, description, status, priority, type, labels, estimate }
- BeadResult interface: { beadId: string, status: 'success' | 'partial' | 'blocked', summary: string }
- BeadReview interface: { beadId: string, passed: boolean, feedback: string, revisionInstructions?: string }

Add NEW fields to OrchestratorState (keep old ones for now):
- activeBeadIds?: string[] — bead IDs created for this orchestration
- beadResults?: Record<string, BeadResult>
- beadReviews?: Record<string, BeadReview>
- currentBeadId?: string | null

Add new phases to OrchestratorPhase:
- 'creating_beads' — LLM is calling br create
- 'refining_beads' — Phase 6 interactive refinement loop
- 'awaiting_bead_approval' — user reviews bead list before execution

DO NOT remove PlanStep, Plan, or any existing state fields yet. That happens in the final cleanup bead.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bead, BeadResult, BeadReview interfaces defined
- New state fields added to OrchestratorState
- New phases added to OrchestratorPhase
- createInitialState() updated with new field defaults
- Existing PlanStep/Plan types still present (no breakage)
- npm run build passes

title: Remove orch_plan tool, add bead approval + Phase 6 refinement + execution launch
description:
This replaces the entire orch_plan registerTool block. The new flow:

1. BEAD APPROVAL: After the LLM creates beads (phase creating_beads), the orchestrator reads br list --json and presents them to the user. UI options:
   - 'Start implementing' → proceed to execution
   - 'Refine beads' → Phase 6 refinement (interactive)
   - 'Reject' → stop

2. PHASE 6 REFINEMENT (interactive): Emit beadRefinementPrompt() telling the LLM to review beads via br list, refine with br update, check with bv. After each pass, re-present the approval UI. User triggers each pass manually (not automated).

3. EXECUTION LAUNCH: On approval, read br ready --json for first batch of executable beads. Determine parallel groups from bead dep graph. Launch execution via parallel_subagents (keyed by bead ID, not step index).

Implementation approach: This is a NEW tool or event handler — not a modification of orch_plan (which gets deleted). Could be:
- A new orch_approve_beads tool the LLM calls after creating beads
- Or automatic: the orchestrator detects phase=creating_beads completion and triggers approval UI

The automatic approach is better — fewer tool calls, less chance of the LLM forgetting.

How to detect completion: After the LLM's response ends while phase=creating_beads, inject a before_agent_start hook or use pi.on('after_turn') to read br list --json and transition to awaiting_bead_approval.

Acceptance criteria:
- orch_plan registerTool block completely removed
- Bead approval UI shows br list output with deps
- Phase 6 refinement is interactive (user-triggered passes)
- On approval, br ready --json determines first executable batch
- Execution launch works with bead IDs (parallel_subagents keyed by bead ID)
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite beads.ts: remove createBeadsFromPlan, add readBeads/readyBeads/beadToTask helpers
description:
beads.ts currently has createBeadsFromPlan() which translates PlanSteps → beads. This is backwards — beads should be created directly by the LLM via br create.

Replace with:
- readBeads(pi, cwd): parse br list --json, return Bead[]
- readyBeads(pi, cwd): parse br ready --json, return Bead[] (open, unblocked, not deferred)
- getBeadById(pi, cwd, id): br get <id> --json → Bead
- beadDeps(pi, cwd, id): br dep list <id> --json → string[] (parent bead IDs)
- updateBeadStatus(pi, cwd, id, status) — already exists, keep
- syncBeads(pi, cwd) — already exists, keep
- validateBeads(pi, cwd) — already exists, keep  
- getBeadsSummary — update to work from Bead[], not stepIndex map

Remove:
- createBeadsFromPlan() entirely

Acceptance criteria:
- No reference to PlanStep in beads.ts
- readBeads and readyBeads return Bead[] from br CLI
- All helpers use pi.exec('br', ...) with --json flag
- validateBeads still checks for cycles via br dep cycles

title: Rewrite prompts.ts: remove plannerInstructions, add bead-creation prompts from Idea Wizard phases
description:
prompts.ts has ~15 functions, many using PlanStep. Full inventory of changes:

REMOVE:
- plannerInstructions() — replaced by beadCreationPrompt
- planToTasksInstructions() — beads ARE tasks, no conversion needed
- parallelExecutionInstructions() — parallel groups now come from br ready

REWRITE to use Bead instead of PlanStep:
- implementerInstructions(step: PlanStep, ...) → implementerInstructions(bead: Bead, ...)
- reviewerInstructions(step: PlanStep, ...) → reviewerInstructions(bead: Bead, ...)
- adversarialReviewInstructions(step: PlanStep, ...) → adversarialReviewInstructions(bead: Bead, ...)
- crossAgentReviewInstructions(goal, steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- realityCheckInstructions(goal, steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- commitStrategyInstructions(steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- summaryInstructions(goal, steps: PlanStep[], results: StepResult[]) → use Bead[] + BeadResult[]
- skillExtractionInstructions() — no PlanStep dependency, keep as-is
- polishInstructions() — no PlanStep dependency, keep as-is

ADD:
- beadCreationPrompt(goal, repoContext, constraints): Phase 5 exact wording from Idea Wizard skill. Tells LLM to use br create / br dep add directly. Must include:
  * br create syntax with --description, --parent, -t, -p flags
  * br dep add syntax
  * Instruction to make beads self-documenting (background, reasoning, acceptance criteria)
  * Scope: each bead should specify which files it touches (in description or labels)
- beadRefinementPrompt(): Phase 6 exact wording. Tells LLM to review via br list, refine with br update, validate with br dep cycles. Includes 'DO NOT OVERSIMPLIFY' constraint.

KEEP UNCHANGED:
- formatRepoProfile(), discoveryInstructions(), orchestratorSystemPrompt(), goalRefinementPrompt()

UPDATE:
- orchestratorSystemPrompt(): change workflow step 4 from 'call orch_plan' to 'create beads via br create'. Remove all orch_plan references. Update system prompt to describe bead-centric workflow.

Acceptance criteria:
- plannerInstructions, planToTasksInstructions, parallelExecutionInstructions removed
- All remaining prompt functions use Bead/BeadResult instead of PlanStep/StepResult
- beadCreationPrompt includes Phase 5 exact wording
- beadRefinementPrompt includes Phase 6 exact wording
- orchestratorSystemPrompt describes bead workflow, not orch_plan workflow
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite orch_select and custom-goal path to emit bead-creation prompts
description:
After orch_select picks a goal (or custom goal is entered), the flow currently calls plannerInstructions() and tells the LLM to call orch_plan. Replace with:

1. orch_select: After goal selection, emit beadCreationPrompt() telling the LLM to create beads via 'br create' / 'br dep add' in bash. Set phase to 'creating_beads'.

2. Custom goal path (in orch_profile, the pencil-emoji branch): Same change — emit beadCreationPrompt() instead of plannerInstructions().

3. Deep plan mode: Instead of spawning 3 LLMs that produce PlanStep arrays, spawn 3 LLMs that each create beads via br create. The synthesis step merges/deduplicates the best beads.

4. Creative brainstorm mode: Brainstorm agents produce ideas as text (no change). The user picks ideas, then the LLM creates beads for the selected ideas.

The key pattern: the LLM calls br create/br dep add directly in bash. No orch_plan tool. The orchestrator detects bead creation by reading br list --json after the LLM finishes.

Acceptance criteria:
- orch_select returns beadCreationPrompt instead of plannerInstructions
- Custom goal path returns beadCreationPrompt instead of plannerInstructions
- Deep plan mode spawns agents that create beads (not PlanStep arrays)
- Phase transitions to 'creating_beads' after goal selection
- No references to orch_plan in orch_select or orch_profile custom-goal path
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite orch_review to work with bead IDs instead of step indices
description:
orch_review currently takes stepIndex: number and works against state.plan.steps. Rewrite to:

PARAMETER CHANGES:
- beadId: string instead of stepIndex: number (primary identifier)
- summary, verdict, feedback, revisionInstructions — same as before

ON PASS:
- updateBeadStatus(beadId, 'closed') via beads.ts
- syncBeads()
- Merge worktree if applicable (use bead ID to find worktree, not step index)
- Agent-mail: send review result as message in bead's thread (thread_id = beadId)

ON FAIL:
- Keep bead open (don't update status)
- Record in state.beadReviews[beadId]
- Increment retry count

PARALLEL GROUP ADVANCEMENT:
After a bead passes, call readyBeads() to check if new beads became unblocked. If yes, launch them.
This replaces the analyzeParallelGroups group-tracking logic entirely — br ready IS the group computation.

GUIDED GATES (runGuidedGates):
Currently references state.plan!.steps and allArtifacts from PlanStep. Rewrite to:
- Get all artifacts from bead descriptions (parse file paths) or from beadIds list
- Replace step-index references with bead IDs in agent task prompts
- Sentinel value for entering guided gates: use a special beadId like '__gates__' instead of stepIndex = steps.length + 1

HIT-ME AGENTS:
- hitMeTriggered and hitMeCompleted keyed by bead ID, not step index
- Agent task prompts reference bead description, not step description
- Agent-mail thread_id = beadId for review threads

IMPORTANT: The 'looks good → advance' logic (lines ~2140-2300) is the most complex part.
Current flow: find unreviewed steps → check parallel groups → launch next group → or advance sequentially.
New flow: readyBeads() → if any ready → launch them (parallel if multiple, sequential if one). Much simpler.

Acceptance criteria:
- orch_review parameter schema uses beadId: string not stepIndex: number
- Pass → updateBeadStatus closed, Fail → stays open  
- syncBeads called after every review
- Advancement uses readyBeads() instead of analyzeParallelGroups
- Guided gates work with bead IDs
- Hit-me agents reference bead IDs
- npm run build passes

title: Rewrite parallel execution to use br ready + agent-mail, remove analyzeParallelGroups
description:
Replace analyzeParallelGroups (in sophia.ts) with br ready-based execution.

REMOVE from sophia.ts:
- analyzeParallelGroups() function  
- resolveDependencies() helper (only used by analyzeParallelGroups)
- ParallelAnalysis interface
- All PlanStep imports in sophia.ts

REMOVE from agent-mail.ts:
- groupArtifactsAreDisjoint(group, steps: PlanStep[]) — rewrite to work with Bead[]

REMOVE from index.ts:
- parallelAnalysis variable and all references
- sameDirGroups tracking
- isSameDirStep() helper

NEW EXECUTION LOOP (in index.ts or new execution.ts):
The execution loop becomes radically simpler:

  while true:
    ready = readyBeads(pi, cwd)
    if ready.length === 0: break  // all done or all blocked
    
    if ready.length === 1:
      // Sequential: implement single bead
      emit implementerInstructions(ready[0])
    else:
      // Parallel: check artifact overlap
      if artifactsDisjoint(ready) && agentMailAvailable:
        launch same-dir with file reservations
      else:
        launch with worktrees
    
    wait for completion
    orch_review each bead

ARTIFACT EXTRACTION from beads:
Beads don't have a structured 'artifacts' field like PlanStep did. Options:
1. Parse file paths from bead description (fragile)
2. Use bead labels as artifact tags (e.g. labels: ['file:src/types.ts', 'file:src/beads.ts'])
3. Require beadCreationPrompt to instruct LLM to put artifacts in a structured section of the description

Option 3 is best: the bead creation prompt tells the LLM to include '### Files: src/foo.ts, src/bar.ts' in every bead description. The orchestrator parses this section.

Acceptance criteria:
- analyzeParallelGroups, resolveDependencies, ParallelAnalysis removed from sophia.ts
- groupArtifactsAreDisjoint rewritten for Bead[] in agent-mail.ts
- Execution uses readyBeads() loop
- Artifact extraction from bead descriptions works
- WorktreePool still works as fallback for overlapping artifacts
- npm run build passes

title: Update session restore and /orchestrate-status to work with beads
description:
Session restore currently rebuilds state from PlanStep data. Update to:

Session restore:
- Read bead state from br list --json (beads survive crashes — they're on disk)
- Reconstruct beadResults/beadReviews from bead statuses
- Determine current phase from bead states: all open = creating_beads, some in_progress = implementing, all closed = complete
- No need to persist plan in session state — beads ARE the persisted plan

/orchestrate-status:
- Show bead summary from br list --json (open/in_progress/closed counts)
- Show current phase
- Show ready beads (next to execute)
- Show coordination backend info

This is actually simpler than current restore because beads are the source of truth, not session JSON.

Acceptance criteria:
- Session restore reads from br list --json
- Phase reconstruction works from bead statuses
- /orchestrate-status shows bead-based progress
- No references to state.plan in restore or status code
- npm run build passes

title: Write AGENTS.md agent-mail section per-project
description: During orchestrator initialization (when agent-mail is detected), check if the project has an AGENTS.md file. If not, create one. If it exists, check for the '## MCP Agent Mail' section. If missing, append the full agent-mail usage guide section covering: what it is, why it's useful, same-repo usage, cross-repo usage, macros vs granular tools, and common pitfalls. This ensures every project agents work in has the coordination instructions.

title: Thread agent-mail messages through guided gates
description: In the guided gates flow (runGuidedGates), when hasAgentMail is true: have review agents send their findings as agent-mail messages in a thread keyed to the plan goal or CR. This creates an auditable trail of review rounds. The orchestrator should fetch_inbox to check for urgent messages from agents before advancing gates.

title: Convert approved plan steps to beads with dependencies
description: After orch_plan approval (where polishing is done and user clicks 'Start implementing'), if hasBeads is true, create beads via 'br create' for each plan step, then 'br dep add' for dependency edges derived from PlanStep.dependsOn. Store bead IDs in state (beadIds: Record<number, string>) similar to sophiaTaskIds. Update bead status to in_progress when implementation starts, close when orch_review passes.

title: Add agent-mail session bootstrap to parallel agent spawning
description: When hasAgentMail is true and spawning parallel sub-agents (via parallel_subagents), inject macro_start_session and file_reservation_paths calls into each agent's task prompt. Use step artifacts as reservation paths. Thread ID should be the bead ID (e.g. pi-orchestrator-XXX) or step index. Agents should send_message progress updates and release reservations when done. Update the agent task templates in orch_plan and orch_review parallel spawn sections.

Use existing test patterns from goal-refinement.test.ts and memory.test.ts.

title: Extract tools into src/tools/ directory
description:
Extract the 6 tool handlers from index.ts into separate files under src/tools/.

## Tools to extract:
- src/tools/profile.ts — orch_profile (index.ts lines ~497-662)
- src/tools/discover.ts — orch_discover (lines ~663-805)
- src/tools/select.ts — orch_select (lines ~806-992)  
- src/tools/approve.ts — orch_approve_beads (lines ~993-1195)
- src/tools/review.ts — orch_review (lines ~1496-1893)
- src/tools/memory-tool.ts — orch_memory (lines ~1404-1495)

## Pattern
Each file exports a registerXTool function:
```typescript
import type { OrchestratorContext } from '../types.js';
export function registerProfileTool(oc: OrchestratorContext) {
  oc.pi.registerTool({ name: 'orch_profile', ... });
}
```

index.ts calls each register function:
```typescript
registerProfileTool(oc);
registerDiscoverTool(oc);
// etc
```

## Key dependencies per tool:
- profile: scanRepo, detectCoordinationBackend, formatRepoProfile, discoveryInstructions, readMemory
- discover: state.candidateIdeas, CandidateIdea type
- select: runGoalRefinement, extractConstraints, beadCreationPrompt
- approve: readBeads, readyBeads, extractArtifacts, validateBeads, beadRefinementPrompt, implementerInstructions, agentMailTaskPreamble
- review: getBeadById, readyBeads, updateBeadStatus, syncBeads, implementerInstructions, runGuidedGates (from gates.ts), mergeWorktreeChanges, agentMailTaskPreamble
- memory: listMemoryEntries, searchMemory, getMemoryStats

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] src/tools/ directory with 6 files
- [ ] index.ts reduced to <200 lines (imports, context creation, registration calls, event hooks)
- [ ] All tools work exactly as before
- [ ] npm run build passes
- [ ] npm test passes

### Files: src/tools/profile.ts, src/tools/discover.ts, src/tools/select.ts, src/tools/approve.ts, src/tools/review.ts, src/tools/memory-tool.ts, src/index.ts

title: Replace sophia-based checkpointing with beads lifecycle
description: Where the orchestrator currently calls sophia checkpointTask, validateCR, reviewCR, etc., add parallel beads lifecycle calls: 'br update <id> --status in_progress' when starting, 'br close <id>' on review pass, and 'br sync --flush-only' to persist state. Keep sophia calls as fallback when hasBeads is false. The beads path should be the primary coordination path when available.

title: Step 4: Extract command handlers (/orchestrate, /orchestrate-stop, /orchestrate-status, /memory — lines ~450-636) into src/commands.ts. Each exports a handler function taking OrchestratorContext. Index.ts registerCommand calls delegate to imports.
description:
